title: Excel won't open file when use addImage
author: gate5th
body:
I'm trying to add and image (preferably png) to an excel file and the code is not generating any errors, but when I try to open the file in excel I get the error: 

> 

"We found a problem with some content in 'the worksheet filename'. Do you want us to try and recover as much as we can? If you trust the source of this workbook, click Yes."  

Clicking yes results in "Excel completed file level validation and repair. Some parts of this workbook may have been repaired or discarded." and shows the completed workbook but without the image. 

my code: 

`
var sigImg = workbook.addImage({
  filename: __dirname + '/' + 'blnkApp131Pg3.jpg',
  extension: 'jpeg',
  });
  console.log('sigImg', sigImg); //returns 0
  worksheet.addImage(sigImg, 'B2:D6');
`

I'm getting the same error whether I use addImage or addBackgroundImage so that makes me think it's related to workbook.addImage

exceljs: 0.4.7
node: 7.10.0
excel: 2016 MSO (16.0.7927.1024) 32-bit

Please help!

 - MjB

comments.author: dlingo
comments.body: How were you able to install the 0.4.7 package? In npm it is still showing version 0.4.6 as the latest and when I npm install from the github address node tells me the package is not found?

comments.author: gate5th
comments.body: 0.4.7 is on npm if you do npm install --save exceljs@0.4.7. However there's a problem with it and it doesn't work unless you go into package.json inside exceljs in node_modules and modify the main to point to the right file. Mine is (sorta) working with it pointed to "main": "./dist/es5/index.js"
